analysis_metadata.notes: complexity=high caps adopt+adapt at 6; domain_events_product_lifecycle and eav_completeness_validation demoted from P1 to defer to respect cap; total meaningful patterns evaluated: 21 plus 5 anti-patterns

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Product as aggregate root with SKU as child entity
decision_matrix.description: Product is the DDD aggregate root that owns SKUs (variants) as child entities. The Product enforces invariants: unique variant_hash across SKUs, at least one active SKU for published products, and variant attribute consistency. Modifications to SKUs go through the Product aggregate within a single UoW.commit().
decision_matrix.justification: Universal consensus (6 sources). Directly answers key question Q1 (Product vs SKU aggregate boundary). The ORM models exist but zero domain entities exist for Product and SKU — the entire domain layer for Product/SKU is missing. This is the foundational pattern: without the Product aggregate root domain entity, nothing else (events, FSM, pricing VO, hash) can be built.
key_questions_addressed: Является ли Product единственным агрегатным корнем, содержащим SKU как дочерние сущности, или SKU — отдельный агрегат?
Нужен ли optimistic locking (version column) на уровне доменной сущности Product, или только в ORM?
Как генерировать variant_hash для SKU — какой алгоритм хеширования комбинации атрибутов?

effort_breakdown.notes: ORM already exists (no migration). New domain entities, repository interface, command handlers, presentation schemas, DI registration needed.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Product status lifecycle with governed FSM transitions
decision_matrix.description: The product moves through a defined state machine: DRAFT -> ENRICHING -> READY_FOR_REVIEW -> PUBLISHED -> ARCHIVED. Transitions are enforced by guard conditions (e.g., completeness check before READY_FOR_REVIEW). Backward transitions are allowed (ENRICHING -> DRAFT, READY_FOR_REVIEW -> ENRICHING) except PUBLISHED -> DRAFT/ENRICHING (must go through ARCHIVED first). Domain status changes emit ProductStatusChangedEvent.
decision_matrix.justification: Consensus (6 sources). Directly answers key question Q3 (allowed status transitions). The ProductStatus enum is already in the ORM (DRAFT, ENRICHING, READY_FOR_REVIEW, PUBLISHED, ARCHIVED) but no FSM logic exists in the domain layer. Anti-pattern 'unrestricted_status_transitions' is already excluded. Must be part of the Product domain entity being built.
key_questions_addressed: Какие переходы статусов допустимы в жизненном цикле продукта (DRAFT -> ENRICHING -> READY_FOR_REVIEW -> PUBLISHED -> ARCHIVED) и можно ли откатиться назад?
Как реализовать soft-delete продуктов — через поле deleted_at (как в ORM) или через статус ARCHIVED?

modified_entities: Product (domain entity — add FSM methods: start_enriching, submit_for_review, publish, archive, revert_to_draft, revert_to_enriching)
effort_breakdown.notes: ORM enum exists. FSM logic implemented as methods on the Product domain entity. our_status=partial reduces effort by 0.7x but complexity=high multiplies by 1.3x.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: ProductAttributeValue structured pivot table (replacing flat JSONB)
decision_matrix.description: Replace the flat Product.attributes JSONB field with a structured ProductAttributeValue pivot table (product_id, attribute_id, attribute_value_id) for product-level EAV. This enables SQL-based filtering, FK constraints, completeness validation against CategoryAttributeBindings, and GIN-indexed search. Pattern mirrors the existing SKUAttributeValueLink table which already handles the variant-level side correctly.
decision_matrix.justification: 4 sources, addresses key question Q7 (EAV validation). The anti-pattern 'flat_jsonb_attributes_without_schema' directly calls out that Product.attributes JSONB prevents SQL filtering, referential integrity, and completeness validation. A structured pivot is required for the completeness validation pattern to work. The variant-level side (SKUAttributeValueLink) already validates this approach. This is a P0 prerequisite for the EAV validation service.
key_questions_addressed: Как валидировать EAV-атрибуты продукта при сохранении — через CategoryAttributeBinding (required/recommended/optional) или отдельный сервис валидации?

modified_entities: Product (remove JSONB attributes field, add relationship to ProductAttributeValue)
effort_breakdown.notes: Migration required to move existing JSONB data to pivot table. ORM Product model change (remove JSONB column, add relationship). +3 for migration.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Domain events for product lifecycle
decision_matrix.description: The Product aggregate emits domain events on key state changes, persisted atomically in the transactional outbox. Events: ProductCreatedEvent, ProductStatusChangedEvent, ProductPublishedEvent, ProductArchivedEvent, ProductDeletedEvent, SKUAddedEvent, SKURemovedEvent, PriceChangedEvent. Each event carries aggregate_id, event_type, timestamp, and a minimal payload with changed data.
decision_matrix.justification: 3 sources. Addresses key question Q10 (which domain events). The project already has established event patterns (BrandCreatedEvent, AttributeCreatedEvent) with transactional outbox infrastructure. Product events are essential for downstream consumers (search indexing, inventory, notifications). Without events, the Product aggregate is isolated from the rest of the system.
key_questions_addressed: Какие доменные события должен эмитировать Product (ProductCreated, ProductPublished, ProductArchived, SKUAdded, PriceChanged)?
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Implement after Product aggregate root and FSM are complete. Events required before any downstream consumers (search indexing, inventory) can react to product lifecycle changes.

modified_entities: Product (add domain event emission in FSM methods)
effort_breakdown.notes: Events are dataclasses following existing pattern. Low implementation cost per event. Outbox infrastructure already exists.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Price stored on SKU as Money value object
decision_matrix.description: Price belongs to the SKU/Variant level. The domain SKU entity carries a Money value object (amount: int, currency: str ISO-4217) instead of raw scalar fields. compare_at_price is also a Money VO (nullable). Prices are stored as integers in the smallest currency unit (kopecks for RUB) per Stripe best practice. The Product exposes computed min_price/max_price across active SKUs for display.
decision_matrix.justification: 6 sources (consensus). Addresses key question Q2 (price placement and type). The ORM already has price/compare_at_price/currency on SKU as integers — the pattern is largely correct. Adaptation: introduce a Money value object in the domain layer to wrap the existing scalar fields. No schema migration needed, only domain layer addition. Multi-currency full MoneyAmount tables (Medusa/commercetools) are skipped — single-currency RUB marketplace does not need them.
key_questions_addressed: Должна ли цена храниться на уровне SKU (как в текущей ORM-модели) или нужен отдельный Price value object в домене?
decision_matrix.simplifications: Simplify from Medusa/commercetools multi-currency MoneyAmount table to a single Money value object (amount+currency) on SKU. Only single currency (RUB) supported initially. compare_at_price is a nullable Money VO. No PriceList or tiered pricing in MVP.

modified_entities: SKU (domain entity — price and compare_at_price become Money VOs)
effort_breakdown.notes: ORM already stores integers correctly. No DB change. Pure domain-layer addition. our_status=partial reduces effort by 0.7x, complexity=high multiplies by 1.3x.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: EAV attribute completeness validation service
decision_matrix.description: A domain service (ProductAttributeValidator) validates that a Product has values for all REQUIRED attributes defined in CategoryAttributeBinding for its primary_category_id. Variant-level validation checks that each SKU has values for all REQUIRED variant-level attributes. Validation is enforced as a guard condition on the ENRICHING -> READY_FOR_REVIEW status transition. Completeness is computed as a percentage: filled_required / total_required * 100.
decision_matrix.justification: 4 sources. Addresses key question Q7 (EAV validation). Adapted from Akeneo's full completeness system: simplified to only block the ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW transition rather than computing a continuous percentage score. The project already has CategoryAttributeBinding with RequirementLevel (REQUIRED/RECOMMENDED/OPTIONAL). This pattern directly leverages that existing infrastructure. Depends on product_attribute_value_pivot being adopted first.
key_questions_addressed: Как валидировать EAV-атрибуты продукта при сохранении — через CategoryAttributeBinding (required/recommended/optional) или отдельный сервис валидации?
decision_matrix.simplifications: Adapt from Akeneo's continuous completeness percentage to a binary gate: all REQUIRED attributes must be filled to transition ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW. RECOMMENDED attributes do not block the transition but are tracked. No channel/locale-scoped completeness needed in MVP.
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Implement after ProductAttributeValue pivot table is in place and Product CRUD is working. Required before ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW transition guard can be enforced.

modified_entities: Product (FSM guard on submit_for_review calls validator)
effort_breakdown.notes: Domain service requires ICategoryAttributeBindingRepository and IProductAttributeValueRepository injected. Must be called from the submit_for_review command handler, not inside the entity (to keep domain entity free of repository dependencies).

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Optimistic locking version field in domain entities
decision_matrix.description: The Product and SKU domain entities carry a version field (int). On load from repository, the current DB version is mapped to the entity. The presentation layer exposes version in GET responses and requires it in PUT/PATCH requests to detect concurrent modifications. On version mismatch, the repository raises a domain-specific ConcurrencyError exception (mapped to HTTP 409).
decision_matrix.justification: 3 sources. Addresses key question Q6 (optimistic locking domain vs ORM). The ORM already has version_id_col on both Product and SKU — the DB enforcement side is done. Adaptation: add version field to domain entities and surface it through the presentation layer. Simplified from commercetools' mandatory version-in-request (we use it on write endpoints only, not required on read endpoints).
key_questions_addressed: Нужен ли optimistic locking (version column) на уровне доменной сущности Product, или только в ORM?
decision_matrix.simplifications: Adapt from commercetools' API-mandatory version requirement to optional ETag-based approach for MVP. Version exposed in responses; update requests accept optional version for conflict detection. ConcurrencyError raised in repository on stale version mismatch.

modified_entities: Product (domain entity — add version: int field)
SKU (domain entity — add version: int field)
effort_breakdown.notes: ORM already enforces version_id_col. Pure domain + presentation addition. our_status=partial applies.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: SHA-256 deterministic variant hash for SKU uniqueness
decision_matrix.description: A deterministic hash of the SKU's variant attribute values ensures no two SKUs within a product have the same attribute combination. Algorithm: 1) collect all variant-level (attribute_id, attribute_value_id) pairs for the SKU, 2) sort by attribute_id (UUID hex lexicographic), 3) join as canonical string '{product_id}|{attr1_id}:{val1_id}|{attr2_id}:{val2_id}', 4) SHA-256 hash truncated to 64 hex chars. Computed by the Product aggregate when adding/updating an SKU.
decision_matrix.justification: 2 sources but relevance=high. Addresses key question Q9 (variant_hash algorithm). The SKU ORM already has a variant_hash String(64) column with a partial unique constraint. The domain layer has no method to compute this hash — it is a missing invariant-enforcement mechanism. Adaptation: implement as a method on the Product entity (not SKU) since Product needs to check uniqueness across all its sibling SKUs.
key_questions_addressed: Как генерировать variant_hash для SKU — какой алгоритм хеширования комбинации атрибутов?
decision_matrix.simplifications: Use Python's built-in hashlib.sha256 (stdlib, no external deps). Include product_id prefix in hash input for global uniqueness. Uniqueness check performed in the Product entity's add_sku method before committing the hash.

modified_entities: Product (domain entity — add compute_variant_hash() and enforce uniqueness in add_sku/update_sku methods)
effort_breakdown.notes: hashlib is stdlib — no infrastructure import. Method placed on Product (aggregate root) to enforce the invariant across sibling SKUs.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Denormalized product read models for CQRS query side
decision_matrix.description: Separate read models (DTOs) for: product list/search queries (flat, denormalized), product detail page, storefront product card. These join Product + SKUs + Attributes + Category + Brand at the DB level and return flat Pydantic read models. Never load the full aggregate for read-only queries. MediaAssets loaded separately and lazily.
decision_matrix.justification: 3 sources (medium relevance). Addresses CQRS read-side concern but is not blocking for MVP write operations. The project already has existing read models (StorefrontFilterListReadModel) and the CQRS infrastructure. Product read models can be added after the core Product aggregate and command handlers are in place. Deferring to v1.1 keeps the P0 scope tight.
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Implement after Product CRUD command handlers are complete and tested. Essential before any storefront-facing product listing or search endpoint is built.

effort_breakdown.notes: Deferred. Will be implemented in v1.1 iteration after write side is stable.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: MediaAsset as domain child entity with ordered roles
decision_matrix.description: MediaAsset is a child entity within the Product aggregate. Carries: media_type, role (MAIN/HOVER/GALLERY/HERO_VIDEO/SIZE_GUIDE/PACKAGING), sort_order, storage_object_id (soft-link to Storage module), optional color binding via attribute_value_id, and external URL flag. Product enforces: one MAIN image per color variant, sort_order integrity.
decision_matrix.justification: 5 sources, high relevance. ORM model exists with all the right fields. However, MediaAsset is not a blocking dependency for the core Product aggregate CRUD — it can be managed separately. Deferring to keep MVP scope within the complexity=high cap of 6 adopt+adapt. Unique constraint (one MAIN per color) is already enforced at DB level. Domain entity can be added in v1.1.
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Add MediaAsset domain entity and presentation endpoints after Product CRUD command handlers are stable. Required before product gallery management feature is built.

effort_breakdown.notes: Deferred. ORM exists. External integration: Storage module soft-link via storage_object_id.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Supplier as minimal domain entity
decision_matrix.description: Supplier is promoted from ORM-only to a minimal domain entity with basic CRUD (name, type, region). The Product aggregate references supplier_id as an opaque UUID FK — it does not need Supplier business rules. Supplier CRUD is a separate command flow, not part of the Product aggregate.
decision_matrix.justification: 2 sources (medium relevance). Addresses key question Q8 (Supplier as domain entity). The competitive report concludes supplier_id as an opaque FK is sufficient for the Product feature scope. Supplier domain entity and CRUD endpoints are not blocking for Product management. Deferring to keep within complexity cap.
key_questions_addressed: Нужен ли Supplier как доменная сущность (сейчас есть только ORM-модель) или достаточно supplier_id как FK?
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Build Supplier domain entity and CRUD endpoints when supplier-facing portal features are needed (marketplace vendor onboarding). For now, supplier_id is referenced as an opaque UUID on Product.

effort_breakdown.notes: Deferred. ORM exists. Straightforward CRUD but not blocking.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Multi-category product assignment (M:N ProductCategoryAssignment)
decision_matrix.description: A separate ProductCategoryAssignment table (product_id, category_id, sort_order, is_primary) enables a product to appear in multiple categories. primary_category_id remains on Product for the canonical URL and attribute schema. Additional categories drive cross-category discoverability.
decision_matrix.justification: 2 sources (medium relevance). Addresses key question Q4 (multi-category). The competitive report recommends starting with primary_category_id only for MVP — sufficient for attribute validation and storefront navigation. Multi-category adds complexity without delivering core value in v1. Defer until merchandising requirements emerge.
key_questions_addressed: Поддерживает ли система несколько категорий для одного продукта (multi-category) или только primary_category_id?
decision_matrix.defer_trigger: Implement when merchandising team requires cross-category product placement. primary_category_id (existing ORM field) is sufficient for MVP.

effort_breakdown.notes: Deferred. No migration needed (additive table). primary_category_id stays as the canonical category.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Multi-currency pricing via MoneyAmount table
decision_matrix.description: Per-variant, per-currency pricing via a separate MoneyAmount entity (variant_id, amount, currency_code, region_id). Enables true multi-currency and region-specific pricing for international marketplaces.
decision_matrix.justification: Single-currency (RUB) marketplace. The competitive report explicitly notes this is overkill for the project's current needs. The existing single Money VO on SKU (amount+currency) is sufficient. Multi-currency would require significant infrastructure (currency conversion, region management, price resolution logic) that has no business requirement.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: solves a problem we do not have (single-currency RUB).

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Staged vs current product data projections
decision_matrix.description: Separate staged (draft changes) and current (published live) projections per product. Allows editing a published product without affecting the live storefront. Publishing copies staged to current.
decision_matrix.justification: Single source. Enterprise-grade pattern for large editorial teams. The project's status-based lifecycle (DRAFT->PUBLISHED) is simpler and sufficient. The dual-projection pattern would require doubling the Product data structure and complicates reads significantly. No stated business requirement for editing published products without affecting the live version.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: single source, enterprise complexity, no current business requirement.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: GS1 GTIN/EAN/UPC barcode field on SKU
decision_matrix.description: SKU carries a barcode field following GS1 standards for interoperability with logistics, retail, and marketplace systems. Field validates EAN-13 / UPC-A / GTIN-14 formats.
decision_matrix.justification: GS1 standard applies 'when products are sold through physical retail or external marketplaces'. Current scope is internal catalog management. sku_code already serves as the primary SKU identifier. Barcode can be added when marketplace integration is a stated requirement.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: not a current business requirement. Can be added as a simple nullable field on SKU when marketplace integration is needed.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Schema.org Product structured data compliance
decision_matrix.description: Product API responses include sufficient data to generate Schema.org Product structured data for SEO (name, description, image, sku, offers with price/currency/availability).
decision_matrix.justification: Applies 'when products are displayed on web storefronts indexed by search engines'. This is a presentation-layer concern for the frontend, not a backend API design concern. The backend will expose all required fields; the frontend renders the structured data markup. No backend changes needed.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: frontend concern. Backend exposes the data; frontend generates the markup.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: ISO 4217 currency codes on price fields
decision_matrix.description: Currency field uses 3-letter ISO 4217 codes (RUB, USD, EUR). Validated at presentation layer.
decision_matrix.justification: Already implemented. SKU.currency is String(3) with 'RUB' default. No action needed.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: already implemented (our_status=exists).

decision_matrix.pattern_name: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes for country_of_origin
decision_matrix.description: Product.country_of_origin uses 2-letter ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 codes (RU, CN, US). Validated at presentation layer.
decision_matrix.justification: Already implemented. Product.country_of_origin is String(2). Add validation in Pydantic schema (min_length=2, max_length=2, uppercase) when presentation layer is built. No action beyond existing ORM.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: already implemented (our_status=exists). Pydantic validation to be added in presentation layer.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Money as integer in smallest currency unit
decision_matrix.description: Prices stored as integers (kopecks for RUB, cents for USD) to avoid floating-point precision errors.
decision_matrix.justification: Already implemented. SKU.price and SKU.compare_at_price are Integer columns. No action needed.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: already implemented (our_status=exists).

decision_matrix.pattern_name: Slug as URL-safe unique identifier
decision_matrix.description: Every product has a URL-safe slug (handle) that is unique among non-deleted products. Used for SEO-friendly URLs. Uniqueness enforced via partial unique index (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL).
decision_matrix.justification: Already implemented. Product ORM has slug with partial unique index uix_products_slug (WHERE deleted_at IS NULL). Domain entity needs a slug field added when Product domain entity is created — handled as part of product_aggregate_root adoption.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: already implemented at ORM level (our_status=exists). Surfaced in product_aggregate_root domain entity as a field.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: ARCHIVED status vs deleted_at as two distinct concepts
decision_matrix.description: ARCHIVED is a business status (intentionally hidden from storefront, reversible, visible in admin). deleted_at is a technical soft-delete (removed from catalog, filtered out everywhere, preserved for order history FK integrity). These coexist on the Product aggregate.
decision_matrix.justification: Both ARCHIVED (ProductStatus enum) and deleted_at (TIMESTAMP) already exist on the ORM model. The FSM domain logic (product_status_lifecycle_fsm adoption) will enforce ARCHIVED as a status transition. soft_delete is implemented via deleted_at in the repository's delete() method. No separate pattern needed — covered by product_aggregate_root and product_status_lifecycle_fsm.

effort_breakdown.notes: Skipped: covered by product_aggregate_root adoption + product_status_lifecycle_fsm adoption. Both ORM fields already exist.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: [ANTI-PATTERN] Storing product attributes as flat JSONB without schema enforcement
decision_matrix.description: Using Product.attributes JSONB as the sole storage for EAV attribute values.
decision_matrix.justification: AUTO-SKIP: listed in anti_patterns. Addressed by adopting product_attribute_value_pivot.

effort_breakdown.notes: Anti-pattern. Eliminated by product_attribute_value_pivot adoption.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: [ANTI-PATTERN] SKU/Variant as a separate aggregate root
decision_matrix.description: Treating SKU as an independent aggregate root with its own repository and lifecycle.
decision_matrix.justification: AUTO-SKIP: listed in anti_patterns. SKU must remain a child entity within the Product aggregate.

effort_breakdown.notes: Anti-pattern. Product is aggregate root, SKU is child entity.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: [ANTI-PATTERN] Unrestricted status transitions (any -> any)
decision_matrix.description: Allowing any status to transition to any other without guard conditions.
decision_matrix.justification: AUTO-SKIP: listed in anti_patterns. Eliminated by product_status_lifecycle_fsm adoption.

effort_breakdown.notes: Anti-pattern. Addressed by governed FSM in product_status_lifecycle_fsm.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: [ANTI-PATTERN] Storing prices as floating-point numbers
decision_matrix.description: Using float/decimal for price storage leading to IEEE 754 precision issues.
decision_matrix.justification: AUTO-SKIP: listed in anti_patterns. Already avoided (SKU.price is Integer). price_on_sku_money_vo adoption reinforces this.

effort_breakdown.notes: Anti-pattern. Already correctly avoided in existing ORM.

decision_matrix.pattern_name: [ANTI-PATTERN] Loading entire aggregate with all variants and media for every query
decision_matrix.description: Eager-loading all Product + SKUs + MediaAssets for list/search queries regardless of use case.
decision_matrix.justification: AUTO-SKIP: listed in anti_patterns. Addressed by adopting product_read_model_cqrs pattern (deferred to v1.1) and the project's CQRS separation of command/query handlers.

effort_breakdown.notes: Anti-pattern. Addressed via CQRS read models.

adopt.one_liner: Create Product and SKU domain entities with Product as aggregate root enforcing all invariants.
Add governed FSM methods on Product entity for DRAFT/ENRICHING/READY_FOR_REVIEW/PUBLISHED/ARCHIVED transitions.
Replace Product.attributes JSONB with a ProductAttributeValue pivot table (product_id, attribute_id, attribute_value_id).

adapt.one_liner: Introduce a Money value object (amount+currency) in the domain layer; SKU entity uses Money for price and compare_at_price.
adapt.what_changed: Simplified from multi-currency MoneyAmount table (Medusa/commercetools) to a single Money VO. No DB migration needed — ORM already stores integers.

adapt.one_liner: Add version: int field to Product and SKU domain entities; expose in API responses; raise ConcurrencyError on mismatch.
adapt.what_changed: Adapted from commercetools mandatory version-in-request to optional version field. ORM enforcement (version_id_col) already exists.

adapt.one_liner: Product.add_sku() computes SHA-256 variant hash from sorted (product_id, attribute_id, value_id) tuples; enforces uniqueness across siblings.
adapt.what_changed: hashlib.sha256 (stdlib) used — no infrastructure import. Hash scoped with product_id prefix for global uniqueness.

defer.trigger: Implement after Product CRUD command handlers are complete. Required before any storefront-facing product listing endpoints.
defer.one_liner: Denormalized read models for product list, detail, and storefront card queries.

defer.trigger: Implement after Product CRUD is stable. Required before product gallery management feature.
defer.one_liner: MediaAsset domain child entity with FSM-guarded gallery management and Storage module soft-link.

defer.trigger: Implement when supplier-facing portal or marketplace vendor onboarding is a stated business requirement.
defer.one_liner: Minimal Supplier domain entity with CRUD; Product references supplier_id as opaque FK in MVP.

defer.trigger: Implement when merchandising team requires cross-category product placement beyond primary_category_id.
defer.one_liner: ProductCategoryAssignment junction table for M:N product-category relationships.

defer.trigger: After Product aggregate root and FSM are complete; required before downstream consumers react to lifecycle changes.
defer.one_liner: ProductCreatedEvent, ProductStatusChangedEvent, SKUAddedEvent, and other lifecycle events emitted by Product aggregate.

defer.trigger: After ProductAttributeValue pivot is in place and Product CRUD is working; required before ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW guard is enforced.
defer.one_liner: ProductAttributeValidator domain service enforcing all REQUIRED attributes filled before status transition.

skip.reason: Single-currency (RUB) marketplace. No business requirement for multi-currency.
Single source, enterprise complexity, no stated business requirement for editing published products without affecting live storefront.
No current marketplace integration requirement. sku_code serves as primary SKU identifier.
skip.one_liner: GS1 GTIN/EAN/UPC barcode field on SKU.

skip.reason: Frontend concern — backend already exposes all required fields.

skip.one_liner: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country codes — already exists.

skip.reason: Already implemented (SKU.price, SKU.compare_at_price are Integer columns).
skip.one_liner: Integer money storage in smallest currency unit — already exists.

skip.reason: Already implemented (Product ORM has slug + partial unique index). Covered by product_aggregate_root domain entity creation.
skip.one_liner: Slug as unique URL-safe identifier — already exists.

skip.reason: Both ARCHIVED (ORM enum) and deleted_at (ORM TIMESTAMP) already exist. Logic covered by product_aggregate_root + product_status_lifecycle_fsm.
skip.one_liner: ARCHIVED vs deleted_at as distinct concepts — already exists in ORM, covered by FSM adoption.

dependencies.note: FSM methods live on the Product domain entity; entity must be created first.
Events are emitted by the Product domain entity. Entity must exist before events can be emitted.
ProductStatusChangedEvent and ProductPublishedEvent are triggered by FSM transition methods.
Hash computation is a method on the Product entity; checks uniqueness across sibling SKUs.
Money VO is a field on the SKU child entity inside the Product aggregate.
Completeness validation reads ProductAttributeValue pivot rows; pivot must exist for validation to query.
Validation is the guard condition on the ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW transition; FSM must be in place.
Read models query data produced by the Product aggregate. Deferred to v1.1.
MediaAsset is a child entity of Product. Deferred to v1.1.

risks.description: product_attribute_value_pivot requires a data migration: existing Product.attributes JSONB data must be moved to the new product_attribute_values table. The JSONB keys are freeform strings, not attribute_id UUIDs — mapping may be lossy if keys do not match attribute codes.
risks.mitigation: Audit current Product.attributes JSONB content before writing migration. If existing data is sparse or test-only, truncate cleanly. If production data exists, write a migration script that maps JSONB keys to Attribute.code lookups. Keep JSONB column as nullable during transition; drop it only after data verification. Use Alembic migration with an explicit data transformation step.

risks.description: complexity=high caps adopt+adapt at 6 patterns. The 11 initially qualifying patterns had to be reduced to 6 by deferring 5 (including media gallery, read models, supplier). If the prd-writer or implementation team includes deferred patterns in MVP scope, the delivery timeline will slip.
risks.mitigation: Strictly enforce the P0 (3 patterns) and P1 (4 patterns) roadmap. Deferred patterns (P2, P3) must not be included in the first implementation sprint. Review pm-spec to ensure MT decomposition respects this boundary.

risks.description: The Product domain entity must be a pure attrs dataclass (zero infrastructure imports per architecture rules). However, variant_hash_algorithm uses hashlib.sha256 — hashlib is stdlib (not infrastructure), so this is valid. The eav_completeness_validation service must NOT be inside the domain entity since it requires repository calls; it must be a domain service injected into the application layer command handler.
risks.mitigation: Validate domain entity purity with the existing architecture boundary tests. The completeness validator is a domain service interface (protocol) injected via Dishka. Repository dependencies stay in the infrastructure layer.

risks.description: Key question Q4 (multi-category) is answered with 'defer primary_category_id only for MVP' but there is no explicit business decision on whether multi-category is a v1.0 or future requirement. If multi-category is needed in v1.0, the Product domain entity design should reserve a secondary_category_ids list field.
risks.mitigation: Product owner must confirm: is multi-category assignment a v1.0 requirement? If yes, add multi_category_assignment to P1 scope and adjust complexity cap accordingly (may require dropping one P1 pattern).

risks.description: MediaAsset (deferred) has a soft-link to the Storage module via storage_object_id. When MediaAsset domain entity is implemented in v1.1, it must coordinate with the Storage module's IStorageRepository without direct cross-module imports. This requires the transactional outbox pattern for any storage operations triggered by media asset changes.
risks.mitigation: When deferral is lifted, design MediaAsset management commands to emit MediaAssetCreatedEvent (via outbox) rather than calling IStorageRepository directly. Storage module consumes the event and manages the file lifecycle.

risks.description: optimistic_locking_in_domain: SQLAlchemy's version_id_col raises StaleDataError (infrastructure exception) on version mismatch. The domain repository must catch this and re-raise as a domain-specific ConcurrencyError to preserve layer isolation.
risks.mitigation: Wrap StaleDataError in the repository implementation's update() method. Define ConcurrencyError in the domain exceptions module. Map to HTTP 409 in the presentation exception handler.

open_questions.question: Is multi-category assignment (ProductCategoryAssignment M:N) a v1.0 business requirement or can it be deferred?
open_questions.context: Key question Q4 is answered with 'start with primary_category_id only, defer multi-category'. However, if the merchandising team needs products to appear in multiple categories at launch, the Product domain entity design must be adjusted now rather than later (additive change, but affects storefront navigation and SEO URL strategy).

open_questions.question: Should the PUBLISHED -> DRAFT backward transition be blocked at the domain level or only discouraged by policy?
open_questions.context: The competitive report recommends blocking PUBLISHED -> DRAFT/ENRICHING (must go through ARCHIVED first). However, admin users may need to quickly pull a product for editing without fully archiving it. The FSM design choice (hard block vs soft warning) affects admin UX.

open_questions.question: What is the exact completeness threshold required for ENRICHING -> READY_FOR_REVIEW transition — all REQUIRED attributes (100%) or a configurable percentage?
open_questions.context: Akeneo allows configuring the completeness threshold per channel/family. The project simplifies to binary (all REQUIRED must be filled). If any category has 0 REQUIRED bindings, completeness is trivially 100% — should this auto-allow the transition, or should a minimum number of attribute values be required regardless?

open_questions.question: Should SKU variant_hash include product_id in the hash input, or rely solely on a composite unique index (product_id, variant_hash)?
open_questions.context: Including product_id in the hash string makes the hash globally unique by construction. Using a separate unique index (product_id, variant_hash) allows the same attribute combination to appear in different products. The competitive report recommends including product_id in the hash. However, this means variant_hash values are not portable across products (cannot use hash to find identical attribute combinations across catalog).

open_questions.question: What is the deletion policy for Product — hard-delete (set deleted_at) or keep permanently for order history? When can deleted_at records be purged?
open_questions.context: The ORM supports soft-delete via deleted_at. The anti-eager-load pattern notes preservation for order history FK integrity. A data retention policy is needed: how long do deleted products stay in the DB before physical deletion? Does the domain delete() method set deleted_at or fully remove the record?

open_questions.question: Should the completeness validation service (ProductAttributeValidator) validate variant-level attributes per SKU, or only product-level attributes?
open_questions.context: Saleor and commercetools validate both levels before publishing. For ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW, should the guard require all active SKUs to have all REQUIRED variant-level attributes filled, or only product-level attributes? Variant-level validation adds complexity but prevents incomplete variants reaching review.

mvp.scope: Build the Product domain entity (aggregate root) with SKU child entities, governed status FSM, and structured ProductAttributeValue pivot table replacing flat JSONB, plus full Product/SKU CRUD endpoints.

v1_0.scope: Add Money value object, optimistic locking, SHA-256 variant hash, domain event emission for all lifecycle transitions, and completeness validation service as guard on ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW.

triggers: domain_events_product_lifecycle: implement after Product aggregate root and FSM are complete; required before search indexing and inventory downstream consumers
eav_completeness_validation: implement after ProductAttributeValue pivot is working; required before ENRICHING->READY_FOR_REVIEW guard is enforced
product_read_model_cqrs: implement after Product CRUD command handlers are complete and tested; required before storefront product listing/search endpoints
media_gallery_domain_entity: implement after Product CRUD is stable; required before product gallery management feature
supplier_domain_entity: implement when supplier-facing portal or marketplace vendor onboarding is a stated requirement
multi_category_assignment: implement when merchandising team requires cross-category product placement beyond primary_category_id
